Comparison

Grade.us vs Revix

Grade.us is a white-label review management platform built for marketing agencies and SEOs who manage reviews on behalf of clients. Revix is an AI review reply engine for restaurant owners who manage their own Google reviews. If you're a restaurant owner evaluating Grade.us, this page explains why a tool built for agencies may not be the best fit — and what to use instead.

Grade.us vs Revix: the short version

Revix

Revix is an AI-powered Google review reply engine. Claude Sonnet writes each response using your restaurant's context (menu, tone, past reviews). Pay per reply: 99 Ft (~$0.29) for positive, 199 Ft (~$0.49) for negative reviews. A typical restaurant pays 3,000-10,000 Ft/month ($8-28). No contract, no setup fee. Connect your Google Business Profile in 10 minutes. Built for independent restaurants, cafes, and hotels managing their own reputation.

Grade.us vs Revix: feature comparison

Grade.usRevix
Price
Pricing model
Contract
White-label dashboard
Review generation (drip campaigns)
Review monitoring
AI review replies
Reply quality (AI model)
Review marketing widgets
Multi-client management
Sentiment analysis
Competitor analysis
Fake review appeal drafts
Guest profiling
Onboarding time
Target user

Grade.us vs Revix: where each wins

Grade.us wins: white-label agency platform

Grade.us is the best white-label review platform for agencies. Replace their branding with yours, give each client a branded dashboard, pull PDF reports with your logo, and manage 100+ client locations from one interface. The white-label add-on costs $440/year or becomes free when you reach 100 seats. No other review tool does white-labeling as well at this price point. If you run an agency, this is Grade.us's strongest advantage.

Grade.us wins: review generation drip campaigns

Grade.us automates review collection with email and SMS drip sequences. Set up a campaign, import customer contacts, and the system sends timed follow-ups asking for reviews on Google, Yelp, or Facebook. Conversion rates typically run 10-15%. Revix doesn't have traditional drip campaigns — it pairs with SpiniX's gamified spin wheel which drives review collection at a 33% rate, but through a different mechanism (in-venue QR scan, not post-visit email drips).

Grade.us wins: review marketing widgets

Grade.us offers SEO-friendly widgets that display your best reviews on your website. These are useful for agencies building client websites who want to showcase social proof. For SEO purposes, structured review data on your domain can help. Revix does not offer website widgets — it focuses on the Google review response side.

Revix wins: AI review replies (Grade.us has none)

This is the biggest gap. Grade.us monitors reviews and helps you collect them, but when a review comes in, you write the reply yourself. There is no AI reply feature. Revix uses Claude Sonnet 4.6 with your restaurant's specific context — menu items, cuisine type, staff names, past review patterns — to generate replies that reference actual dishes and experiences. For a restaurant getting 20-50 reviews per month, that's 20-50 replies you don't have to write.

Revix wins: per-reply pricing for direct business use

Grade.us charges $54/mo per seat regardless of how many reviews you get. A quiet month with 5 reviews costs the same as a busy month with 40. Revix charges per reply: 99 Ft (~$0.29) positive, 199 Ft (~$0.49) negative. A restaurant with 25 reviews/month pays about $9-12. You only pay when the AI writes a response. No flat fee, no wasted spend on low-volume months.

Revix wins: competitor benchmarking and fake review defense

Revix includes local cluster benchmarking (your rating, review velocity, and response rate vs similar businesses within 20 km) and AI-generated fake review appeal drafts citing Google's review policies. Grade.us offers neither. For restaurant owners dealing with competitive local markets or suspicious reviews, these features matter daily.

Grade.us vs Revix: FAQ

Is Grade.us for restaurant owners or agencies?

Grade.us is built for agencies. Its core features — white-label dashboard, multi-client management, branded reporting, and drip campaigns run on behalf of clients — are designed for marketing agencies and SEO firms. A restaurant owner can use it, but you'd be paying for agency features you don't need and still writing every review reply yourself. If you're a restaurant owner managing your own reviews, Revix is the direct-use tool with AI replies included.

Can I white-label Revix for my clients?

No. Revix is designed for direct business use — restaurant owners connect their Google Business Profile and manage their own reviews with AI-generated replies. There is no white-label option, no agency dashboard, and no multi-client management. If you're an agency that needs white-label review management, Grade.us is purpose-built for that. If your agency clients want AI reply quality, you could recommend Revix to them for direct use alongside your agency's other services.

Does Grade.us write AI replies to reviews?

No. Grade.us focuses on review generation (collecting reviews via drip campaigns) and review monitoring (tracking reviews across sites). When a review arrives, you write the response manually. There is no AI reply feature. This is the biggest functional difference from Revix, which auto-generates Claude Sonnet replies for every Google review using your restaurant's context.

How much does Grade.us cost for a single restaurant?

The Professional plan costs $54/month ($648/year) for one seat. This gives you review monitoring, drip campaigns for review collection, and basic reporting — but no AI replies. A restaurant owner using Revix for the same review volume would pay $108-336/year and get AI-generated replies to every review. Grade.us makes more financial sense when you're an agency charging clients $200+/month for reputation management and need the white-label wrapper.

Which tool is better for collecting more reviews?

Grade.us has stronger traditional review generation with email/SMS drip campaigns that achieve 10-15% conversion rates. You import customer contacts and the system sends timed follow-ups directing people to Google, Yelp, or Facebook. Revix pairs with SpiniX's gamified spin wheel for in-venue review collection at a 33% rate — higher conversion but requires a QR scan in the restaurant. Different approaches: Grade.us is post-visit email outreach, SpiniX is in-venue gamification.

Can I use Grade.us and Revix together?

Yes. Some agencies use Grade.us for review generation and monitoring across platforms, then recommend Revix to restaurant clients for AI reply quality on Google reviews. Grade.us collects the reviews; Revix responds to them. This split approach costs more than Revix alone but makes sense for agencies that want white-label reporting plus best-in-class AI replies for their restaurant clients.

Does Grade.us require an annual contract?

No. Grade.us offers monthly billing on all plans — you can cancel month-to-month. This is a genuine advantage over platforms like Birdeye or Podium that require 12-month contracts. Both Grade.us and Revix offer contract-free usage, though Revix has no monthly minimum at all (you only pay per reply).

AI review replies without the agency middleman

Claude Sonnet quality replies tuned to your restaurant. Per-response pricing. No contract. Live in 10 minutes.
